meaning: adj.庄严的,值得尊敬的,古老的
examples: May Day has become a venerable institution.
Her Chinese friends referred to the empress as their venerable ancestor.
Will you please not suspect my venerable ancestors?
This venerable country house hotel on the edge of Elterwater has been given a much-needed makeover.
However, should the venerable DJIA refuse to yield, the current break may not last long.
